title: ED domestic violence screening documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
source.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation
source.population: Women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence; the handbook does not directly address people under 18 or men.
source.setting: Health-care encounters including first-line support, history, examination and documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for survivor-centred first-line support, consent to documentation, recording complaints, symptoms, signs and injuries, and history or examination only when clinically performed. It is not authority for universal screening or automatic reporting in the UAE.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

source.title: Management of Domestic Abuse
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; requires UAE legal and safeguarding adaptation
source.population: Adults presenting to emergency care where domestic abuse is disclosed or clinically suspected; the text focuses primarily on women.
source.setting: Emergency department recognition, confidential disclosure, injury documentation and local safeguarding support.
source.exclusions: Exact only for confidential, clinician-led enquiry where suspicion exists, documentation at disclosure, injury documentation and recording child-welfare concerns. It is not used for universal screening or automatic police, shelter or referral action.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.

assessment.text: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.
assessment.rationale: Recognition and clinically indicated direct enquiry were reviewed; the source does not support universal screening.
